By cutting routine manual work and surfacing the right insights at the right time, it lifts productivity, reduces downtime, and keeps operations running smoothly.

 

Further innovations across various LoBs

SAP Integrated Product Development

Handover from Formulation Recipe to S/4HANA Master Recipe

Bringing new products from development into manufacturing often requires manual handovers and multiple data transfers, increasing the risk of inconsistencies and delays. Handover to Master Recipe in SAP Integrated Product Development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition streamlines this transition by enabling production engineers to derive production-ready master recipes directly from product development data. The innovation automates the creation of master recipes, bills of materials (BOMs), and production versions while preserving the relationship between engineering and manufacturing data. By reducing manual effort and ensuring greater consistency across product development and production planning, organizations can accelerate manufacturing readiness, improve process reliability, and support a more seamless transition from product design to execution. For more information, consult our SAP Help Portal.

 

Sales

Supply block in a sales order to control subsequent activities

A single sales order can trigger a cascade of downstream activities, from available-to-promise (ATP) checks and material requirements planning (MRP) to logistics execution. Supply block in a sales order to control subsequent activities helps organizations ensure these processes only begin when order data is complete, validated, and ready for execution. This innovation introduces an automated, item-level supply block that can be configured based on business rules, approval workflows, credit checks, or custom validation logic. By temporarily pausing subsequent activities until all required validations have been completed, organizations can reduce operational risk, minimize manual intervention, improve visibility into order status, and help ensure more accurate, reliable, and efficient order fulfillment.

 

Environment, Health, and Safety

Maintain and view hazardous substance inventory

Another innovation focuses on workplace safety and regulatory compliance. The new Maintain and View Hazardous Substance Inventory applications provide a centralized view of hazardous substances, associated products, and safety information across the enterprise. Users can create and maintain comprehensive hazardous substance inventories, organize data by business area or location, manage inventory revisions, and quickly access key details such as hazardous ingredients and Globally Harmonized System (GHS) classifications. This helps organizations strengthen compliance, improve workplace safety, and manage safety-critical information more efficiently.

 

Professional Services

New and improved time-recording app for professional services projects with a smooth mobile experience

Let’s close with professional services. The redesigned Time Recording app brings a modern, consistent experience across desktop and mobile, with flexible entry, bulk edits, and personalized views that make logging project time fast and effortless. The payoff is clear: more accurate timesheets, less administrative work, and stronger billing and resource planning.

   

                                         

 

Service

Customer and product hierarchy pricing

Every customer expects repair pricing to be transparent, accurate, and aligned with the agreements they have negotiated. Customer Hierarchy and Product Hierarchy Related Pricing in In-House Repair Transactions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 enables organizations to apply pricing and discounts consistently across in-house repair processes based on customer and product hierarchies. By automatically determining the appropriate pricing conditions for different customer groups and product structures, the innovation eliminates manual pricing adjustments and helps ensure that negotiated agreements are applied correctly throughout the repair process. The result is more accurate invoicing, greater pricing consistency, an improved customer experience, and a smoother connection between service execution and billing.

 

Cross Topics – Data and Analytics

Central governance of product data and business partners including customer and supplier segments

High-quality master data is the foundation of every successful ERP system. The central governance of product data and business partners helps organizations establish trusted product, customer, supplier, and business partner data through workflow-driven governance, configurable validation rules, automated field value derivation, and flexible approval processes powered by SAP Build Process Automation. By improving data quality from the point of entry and reducing manual maintenance, organizations can streamline governance, strengthen compliance, and enable more reliable business processes and better decision-making across the enterprise.

 

 

Smart business runtime environment

The Smart business runtime environment introduces AI-generated summaries that help business users understand KPIs and reports faster. Business users often spend significant time analyzing dashboards, drilling down into reports, and interpreting large volumes of business data before they can identify trends or make informed decisions. With the Smart Business Runtime Environment, users can automatically generate concise, contextual summaries for SAP Smart Business KPIs and reports directly within SAP Fiori, highlighting key insights, anomalies, and important business trends. By transforming complex analytics into clear, shareable narratives, the innovation accelerates insight consumption, simplifies data interpretation, and enables faster, more informed decision-making across the organization.

 

Closing thoughts

Every release is an opportunity to make SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition smarter, simpler, and more valuable for our customers.

On one hand, we’re expanding the possibilities of Business AI with intelligent assistants and specialized agents. On the other, we’re doubling down on the core capabilities that help organizations run better every day.

Those above are my key innovations in the 2608 Release Highlights. I’m keen to hear what matters most to you.

I hope this introduction gave you some appetizers of what’s new — and spotlighted a few innovations you’ll want to explore further. For deeper insights, check out the “What's New in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” SAP Help page and join the conversation on SAP Community to share which innovations you’re most excited about.

In the coming weeks, we'll take a closer look at additional business- and industry-specific innovations in a series of follow-up posts here on SAP Community.  To stay up to date with future content, be sure to follow the PSCC_Enablement tag and keep an eye on upcoming posts.

Feel free to follow me at BeSchulze in the community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates. 

Until next time,

Bert

 

 

🎦Register for our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 Early Release Series! 

Between July 13-17, we are hosting a series of 24 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. On our landing page you can register for the entire series or pick the sessions most relevant to you. 

🔎Find all our assets  

The release 2608 showcases more than 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:

entry.title: How SAP MDG lets you download and fix problem records directly from your process
entry.content:
Introduction

If you have ever run a mass change or consolidation process in SAP Master Data Governance, you know how valuable the validation step is. However, when the validation step stops due to errors, the erroneous records are stuck inside the system. Error messages are visible, but there was no way to extract, fix, and re-upload them.

And when a file upload fails due to invalid source IDs, all you get was a generic message like "Load contains invalid source IDs: 2". No details, no way to tell which records are actually affected. Either way, users end up rebuilding files manually.

This pain point was also one of the most strongly requested topics in SAP Customer Influence, with many customers highlighting the need for a smoother way to handle failed records. This was addressed, both following the same principle: giving a possibility to download a file of the problematic records, in exactly the format needed to fix and re-upload.

Feature 1: Export Process Data with Messages in the Validation Step

The Problem

In a consolidation or mass change process, the validation step checks the record correctness. When errors are found, the process stops and shows aggregated KPIs (e.g., "47 records with errors"). You can drill into the Details UI to see which records failed and why, but there was no way to export that subset for offline correction and re-upload.

What Was Delivered

The details UI of the validation step directly includes a new export action. When pressing the button it navigates to the export app and the filter for selected records is automatically set.

What it does:

  • Exports all records currently displayed in the validation step's overview tab
  • Includes the related messages (errors, warnings) as an additional sheet/section in the exported file
  • Uses the standard MDG export file format, the exported file can be edited offline and re-uploaded directly into a new import or mass processing run

After downloading the file errors and warnings are shown in the ! Messages ! tab of your excel sheet:

Availability

  • MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2022
  • SAP S/4HANA cloud public edition

Feature 2: Download Invalid Records During Upload

The Problem

When uploading a file for a mass process, the system performs a technical validation, e.g. it checks whether the IDs in the file exist in the system. If they do not, it raises a generic error:

"Load contains invalid source IDs: 3"

That is all the users got, just generic information, no more details. Which three records? Which IDs? Users had to manually inspect the file, compare it with the system, and figure it out themselves.

What Was Delivered

Whenever invalid records in a file are detected, it is possible now to export those records from the original file by an “Export”-button in File Import Log area.

The file is in the same format as the upload, so users can correct the records (fix or remove the invalid IDs) and re-upload without rebuilding the file from scratch.

Availability

  • MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 in classic mode
  • MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 in cloud-ready mode
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Why These Two Features Matter Together

Both features address the same need: when something goes wrong in an MDG process, users need a way to act quickly. With both features, you can download the problematic records as a file, fix them offline, and use that file as the starting point for a follow-up process.

Feature 1 helps when errors are found during the validation step. Feature 2 helps even earlier, when the file upload itself fails due to invalid IDs. In both cases, the result is the same: a downloadable file with exactly the records that need to be fixed. This means users no longer must rebuild files from scratch or investigate manually which records caused the error. This saves time, reduces the load on technical teams, and makes the whole process more reliable, especially when working with large data volumes where even a small error rate can mean hundreds of records to fix.

Have you ever used one of these features?

_________________

Further Links and Resources

  • SAP Master Data Governance community https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/master-data-governance
  • SAP Help Portal https://help.sap.com/docs/MASTER_DATA_GOVERNANCE - Various help documents for e.g. MDG on S/4HANA Cloud Private and Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP Influence Portal https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3497 - SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA: Submit new ideas or vote on existing requests to shape the MDG roadmap
  • SAP Learning https://learning.sap.com/courses/sap-master-data-governance-on-sap-s-4hana - Learning content covering MDG strategy, domains and processes related to MDG central governance, consolidation and mass processing end-to-end

entry.title: Work Smarter with AI-Assisted Tasks in the SAP Activate Project Management Workstream
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series highlighting AI capabilities in SAP Activate. For additional information, visit the main blog here. 

Project Managers are expected to balance strategic planning with day-to-day execution while keeping projects on schedule, within budget, and aligned with business objectives. To help streamline these activities, the SAP Activate road map viewer now includes AI-assisted procedures for tasks within the Project Management workstream.

These AI-Assisted procedures are designed to help project managers quickly create high-quality project deliverables by combining SAP Activate best practices with generative AI. Rather than starting from a blank page, project teams can leverage guided AI prompts to accelerate planning, improve consistency, and reduce manual effort.

What Are AI-Assisted Procedures?

AI-Assisted procedures provide step-by-step guidance for using generative AI to complete common project management activities. Each procedure includes a structured prompt that incorporates SAP Activate methodology and best practices, helping generate a tailored starting point that can be reviewed and refined for your specific project.

The generated content is intended to support project teams by providing a solid first draft while allowing project managers to apply their expertise and project-specific knowledge before finalizing the deliverable.

The following Project Management tasks now include AI-Assisted procedures with the SAP Activate road map viewer:

Develop a Project Charter

A well-defined project charter establishes the project’s purpose, objectives, scope, governance, and success criteria. The AI-Assisted procedure helps generate a comprehensive project charter by incorporating information such as project goals, stakeholders, timelines, assumptions, constraints, risks, and governance structure.

This provides project managers with a structured draft that can be customized to meet the organization's specific requirements.

Estimate Backlog Items

Accurate backlog estimation is essential for planning releases and managing project delivery. The AI-Assisted procedure assists teams in developing consistent effort estimates by analyzing backlog item descriptions and applying estimation guidance based on SAP Activate best practices.

The resulting estimates can be reviewed and refined by the project team during backlog and sprint planning activities.

Develop a Hypercare Support Plan

Preparing for a post-go-live support is critical to ensuring a successful transition into operations. The AI-Assisted procedure helps generate a structured Hypercare Support plan that outlines support objectives, roles and responsibilities, issue management processes, communication plans, monitoring activities, escalation paths, and exit criteria.

This provides implementation teams with a comprehensive starting point for planning the Hypercare period.

Handover Operations Responsibility

A smooth transition from the implementation project to the operational support organization is key to long-term success. The AI-Assisted procedure assists project teams in developing an operational handover plan that defines transition activities, knowledge transfer, documentation requirements, support responsibilities, and operational readiness.

The generated plan helps ensure that operational teams receive the information they need to effectively support the solution after go-live.

Communicate Project Status and Progress

Keeping stakeholders informed with timely, accurate, and consistent project updates is essential for effective project governance. The AI-Assisted procedure helps project managers create a comprehensive project status report by generating a structured summary of overall project health, milestone progress, schedule and budget status, key accomplishments, risks and issues, upcoming activities, and decisions requiring stakeholder attention.

This provides project managers with a professional, SAP Activate-aligned starting point that can be reviewed, tailored with project specific information, and shared with stakeholders to support informed decision making and transparent project communication.

Why Use AI-Assisted Procedures?

By incorporating AI-Assisted procedures into the SAP Activate methodology project teams can:

  • Accelerate the creation of common project management deliverables.
  • Start with structured, SAP Activate-aligned content instead of a blank document.
  • Improve consistency across projects by leveraging standardized guidance.
  • Reduce the time spent drafting project documentation.
  • Allow project managers to focus more on project leadership, stakeholder engagement, and decision-making.

These procedures are designed to enhance, not replace, the expertise of experienced project managers. Human review remains an essential part of the process to validate accuracy, tailor the content to the project, and ensure alignment with organizational standards.

The AI-Assisted Procedure tag helps you quickly identify tasks that include guidance for using generative AI. These tasks contain an AI-Assisted procedure and a link to the SAP Activate Prompt Library, making it easy to generate first drafts of project deliverables while following SAP Activate best practices.

entry.title: What’s new in Artificial Intelligence

entry.title: Transform Your Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation with AI-Assisted Procedures
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series highlighting AI capabilities in SAP Activate. For an overview of the complete series, visit the main blog post here: SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions ... - SAP Community

What if AI could help your project team prepare Fit-to-Standard workshops faster, more consistently, and with greater confidence?

Now it can.

Why Fit-to-Standard preparation matters

Preparing for Fit-to-Standard workshops is one of the most important activities in an SAP implementation.

Before the workshops begin, the project team must:

  • Understand and validate the implementation scope.
  • Map the business processes and solutions to be demonstrated.
  • Identify the appropriate stakeholders.
  • Define clear roles and responsibilities.
  • Prepare a detailed workshop agenda.
  • Determine the required inputs and expected outcomes.

This preparation is essential—but it can also require significant time and coordination.

What if an AI-assisted procedure could help the project team perform these activities?

We have exciting news.

AI assistance is now embedded in SAP Activate

SAP Activate now includes AI-Assisted Procedures for selected tasks within the Application: Design and Configuration workstream of the SAP Cloud ERP Private – New Implementation roadmap.

These procedures combine SAP Activate best practices with generative AI to help project teams create comprehensive Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation and planning materials more efficiently.

Each AI-Assisted Procedure is:

  • Embedded directly in the task description in SAP Roadmap Viewer.
  • Connected to a task-specific prompt.
  • Supported by the SAP Activate Prompt Library.
  • Designed to fit naturally into the implementation workflow.

This means project teams can access AI guidance precisely where they need it—without leaving SAP Roadmap Viewer.

Let’s explore “Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops”

Open the Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops task in SAP Roadmap Viewer.

Inside the task, you will find an AI-Assisted Procedure that guides you through the workshop preparation process. Open the procedure and follow steps 1 through 9.

The procedure helps you organize the necessary project information and use the corresponding prompt to generate a structured Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation plan.

Two new accelerators to support the process

We have also created two accelerators to help project teams use the procedure and its prompt more effectively:

  1. Fit-to-Standard Workshop Notes — a structured template for capturing the information required to prepare and run the prompt.
  2. SAP Activate Prompt Library — a collection of prompts supporting multiple activities across the SAP Activate roadmaps, including the Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops prompt.

The SAP Activate Prompt Library is available directly from the SAP Roadmap Viewer. Simply click the prompt referenced in the task to access it.

Each prompt entry provides consultants with the information they need to:

  • Understand the purpose of the prompt.
  • Identify the required inputs.
  • Execute the prompt correctly.
  • Review the expected output before getting started.

A structured prompt based on the COP Framework

The prompts follow the COP Prompt Framework, which is organized around:

  • Context — the project background and information the AI needs.
  • Objectives — the results the prompt should produce.
  • Preferences — the expected structure, level of detail, and output format.

This framework makes the prompts easier to understand, execute, and adapt to the specific needs of each project.

What does the prompt generate?

The Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation Plan prompt can generate:

  1. Scope Validation Checklist — a pre-workshop sign-off checklist for the System Integrator Lead and Customer Project Manager.
  2. Business Process Scoping Matrix — maps in-scope functional areas to SAP Best Practices scope item IDs, workshop dates, and business owners.
  3. Business Stakeholder Pre-Work — an actionable checklist of items due at least five business days before each workshop.
  4. System Readiness Checklist — covers sandbox provisioning, SAP Best Practices activation, master data, user access, and SAP Cloud ALM setup.
  5. Workshop Agenda Templates — includes a general full-day Fit-to-Standard agenda and tailored versions for functional areas such as Finance, Procure-to-Pay, Order-to-Cash, Manufacturing, Warehouse Management, and Asset Management.
  6. Fit-to-Standard Tracking Template — provides a detailed gap log, including one row for each delta requirement identified in the workshop notes.
  7. Roles and RACI Matrix — defines accountability across key customer, system integrator, architecture, consulting, and project management roles.
  8. Gap Capture Guidance for SAP Cloud ALM — provides a step-by-step process for recording gaps, completing mandatory attributes, assigning resolution categories, and maintaining traceability.
  9. Risks and Mitigation Strategies — creates a risk register covering workshop execution, scope, stakeholders, and technical readiness.
  10. Classification Decision Tree — supports consistent classification of requirements as Fit, Partial Fit, Gap, Out of Scope, or Deferred.

A representative example is also available in the Sample Output section of the prompt library.

What are the benefits?

AI-Assisted Procedures can help project teams:

  • Accelerate Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation.
  • Generate agendas based on the project scope.
  • Improve visibility into required resources, stakeholders, and inputs.
  • Reduce the time needed to validate workshop topics and schedules.
  • Establish a structured timetable for the topics to be discussed.
  • Apply SAP Activate guidance more consistently.
  • Start from a comprehensive, structured plan that the project team can review and refine.

AI supports the preparation—but the project team remains responsible for reviewing, validating, and adapting the output to the project context.

This is just the beginning

AI-Assisted Procedures bring SAP Activate guidance and generative AI together directly within the implementation workflow.

And this is only the beginning—more AI-assisted content is on the way.

Are you ready to use the Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation prompt in your next project?

Try it and share your experience in the comments. We would love to learn how you used the prompt, what value it brought to your project, and which SAP Activate task you would like to see supported next.

entry.title: News from SAP Activate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce the updates to SAP Activate, including the availability of AI-Assisted tasks. We’re also excited to share the newly refreshed SAP Activate page on sap.com, which offers a clearer, more intuitive overview of the SAP Activate methodology. 

This release delivers a range of enhancements across SAP Activate assets, strengthening implementation guidance and improving usability. As always, our focus remains on simplifying and optimizing the implementation journey to provide a more seamless experience for our users. 

Below are the key highlights for this release:

  • SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: AI-Assisted Tasks Now Available
  • What’s new in Activate Content Library (ACL)
  • SAP Activate Community page
  • Updates to SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Updates to SAP Activate for SAP Cloud ERP Private
  • Updates to SAP Activate for Conversion of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2-system to 3-system landscape
  • Updates to SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test Automation Tool 2608 Release

SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: AI-Assisted Tasks Now Available

SAP Activate now incorporates SAP Business AI across three dimensions: (1) guiding customers to adopt SAP Joule and Business AI capabilities during implementation, (2) supporting extensions built on the SAP Business AI Platform in line with clean core, and (3) leveraging AI to accelerate implementation activities across the project lifecycle. 

As part of this evolution, the first wave of AI-assisted implementation tasks was released earlier in June and enhanced during July, delivering 18 AI-assisted tasks, 21 new tested prompts, and updated accelerators. The initial focus is on the use of SAP Cloud ALM AI capabilities, SAP Joule for Consultants, and Test Automation Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

They cover a range of project activities — from self-enablement and project artifact preparation to fit-to-standard workshop support, requirements generation, extension design, data migration planning, and testing. You can identify these tasks using the "AI-Assisted Procedure" tag in the SAP Activate road map viewer on SAP for Me, where SAP Joule is also available as a conversational assistant to help you navigate implementation guidance. 

Read the full blog post here for a detailed walkthrough of all three dimensions and how to get started.

SAP Activate Community | SAP Activate Blogs

Join the SAP Activate Community to explore valuable content and resources while connecting with SAP partners, clients, and internal colleagues by participating in discussions and sharing knowledge. Make sure to follow the SAP Activate tag to access blog posts like those highlighted here, along with additional insights shared by SAP Activate experts within the community.

Recap of some blog posts from the community:

Recap of some blog posts from the community:

  • SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions of AI for Your Implementation by Jan Musil
  • Introducing the SAP Activate Prompt Library: Curated AI Prompts for your Implementation Project by Chandan Gurav
  • AI-Assisted Extensibility Planning & Design in SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud by Kiran Bose
  • SAP Activate, AI and Data Management by Daniel Ciecko
  • Work Smarter with AI-Assisted Tasks in the SAP Activate Project Management Workstream by Christina Galbreath
  • Transform Your Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation with AI-Assisted Procedures by Jair Azevedo
  • New SAP Activate Roadmap for SAP Sales Cloud V2 by Chandan Gurav
  • SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors by user alejandroazca
  • Update on SAP Activate Road Map Viewer Accelerators for Central Finance by Carla Nascimento
  • Exploring the New Guided Walk-Thru for SAP Activate for SAP IBP by user MabelleRM
  • Quality Gates: Leveraging the continuous quality check (CQC) Service Report for Project Readiness by Christina Galbreath
  • Foundational News: Implementing SAP Business Data Cloud – New Roadmap Release in SAP Roadmap Viewer by Ariane Hoffman
  • Do you want to boost your SAP project skills with Agile & SAP Activate by Margit Wagner

Updates of SAP Activate for SAP S/HANA Cloud Public Edition in 2608

The SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation roadmap has been enhanced based on product management input, system functionality changes, and customer feedback. Below are highlights for this release.

  • Expanded AI support: AI-assisted procedures have been added across multiple implementation tasks to help customers and consultants accelerate delivery using Business AI and Joule for Consultants. A new AI-Assisted Procedure tag and Prompt Library make AI-enabled guidance and prompts easier to find.
  • Business AI integration: AI-powered scenarios are now embedded throughout the implementation methodology, providing guidance at the point where it is most relevant.
  • Enhanced Fit-to-Standard guidance: New Fit-to-Standard overview video and RACI matrix help project teams better understand workshop activities and stakeholder responsibilities. See task Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops.
  • Integrated Quality Gates: Quality Gates are now incorporated into the Continuous Quality Check (CQC) process, enabling customers to complete phase-end questionnaires directly in SAP Cloud ALM and submit them to SAP.

Improved upgrade methodology: The upgrade roadmap for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape) has been expanded with more detailed guidance and stronger project management support for major upgrade milestones. SAP Activate for the Upgrade of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape).

Updates of SAP Activate for Cloud ERP Private

SAP Activate, the standardized framework for the RISE with SAP Methodology, has been enhanced with numerous updates, as detailed in the release notes.

This release significantly expands AI capabilities by introducing AI-assisted procedures, prompt-based accelerators, and enhanced SAP Cloud ALM guidance that help consultants streamline project execution, improve documentation quality, and increase consistency using SAP Joule for Consultants. In addition, the release strengthens Clean Core integration guidance with the latest SAP recommendations and introduces new AI-powered learning and change management capabilities to accelerate end-user enablement and content development. 

Key highlights include:

  • Expanded SAP Business AI integration across the SAP Activate for SAP Cloud ERP Private methodology, embedding AI-assisted procedures and new prompt-based accelerators into key project lifecycle tasks — including backlog estimation, cutover planning, hypercare support, and operations handover — enabling consultants to accelerate execution and improve output quality with SAP Joule for Consultants.
  • Enhanced SAP Cloud ALM guidance with AI capabilities, adding new procedure steps and accelerators for document summarization and AI-assisted requirement generation across project setup, workshop execution, and requirements documentation tasks, helping project teams work faster and more consistently.
  • Strengthened Clean Core integration guidance by incorporating SAP Note 3690029 — Integration Technologies and Frameworks in Context of Clean Core Integration — across all PCE scenarios, ensuring consultants have up-to-date direction on compliant integration approaches aligned with clean core principles.

Improved learning and change management enablement by introducing AI-assisted accelerators for user learning and change management, including a new micro-app hub integration via SAP Discovery Center, supporting more targeted and efficient learning needs analysis and content development planning.

See the release notes for a complete list of all improvements. 

Updates of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2-system landscape to 3-system landscape 

We are happy to share the highlights of the latest improvements to the SAP Activate guidance for our customers converting their SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition environments from a 2-system landscape to a 2-system landscape.

These are the key updates:

  • Updated assignment of tasks to the “Day 1 Post-Conversion Checklist” tag to include the tasks added to the Realize phase to address specific post-conversion steps.
  • Multiple updates for test data refresh and test data conversion - revised procedure details in the tasks Prepare for Test Data Conversion Service and Prepare for Test Data Refresh Service. Additionally, refreshed the accelerator Test Data Conversion Checklist.
  • Added new task Copy Workforce Users and Request Test Data Refresh after the Conversion in the Realize phase, with additional details for the customer to copy workforce users and submit a request for TDR, including the addition of new accelerators.
  • Updated task Prepare for Test Data Refresh Service in the Prepare phase with information about how to request the service.
  • Updated task Prepare for Test Data Conversion Service in the Prepare phase with information about how to request the service.

Updates of SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors

The SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors roadmap has been enhanced based on product management input, system functionality changes, and customer feedback. Below are the highlights:

  • Integrated Quality Gates: Phase-end Quality Gates are now part of the Continuous Quality Check (CQC) process, allowing questionnaires to be completed and submitted directly through SAP Cloud ALM.
  • Business AI integration: AI-powered scenarios are now embedded throughout the implementation methodology to provide contextual guidance when teams need it.
  • Joule integration: New guidance has been added for configuring and enabling Joule as part of the implementation process. Joule integration.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test Automation Tool 2608 Release

The CE2608 release introduces additional enhancements to simplify test scoping, improve test data management, and enable easier comparison test statuses across different Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) executions.

Key highlights include:

  • AI-Powered Test Scoping in Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS)
    • Enhanced by combining AI with SAP Standard code-change analysis to automatically recommend the most relevant Post Upgrade Test (PUT) scope.
    • Smarter test scope recommendations: The AI model considers customer usage, process complexity, change-to-touch ratio, historical regression behavior, and SAP standard code changes to identify the optimal regression scope.
    • Reduced testing effort and costs: Customers can execute fewer, more relevant regression tests, reducing validation effort, shortening execution time, and lowering overall testing costs without compromising quality.
    • No additional customer action required: AI-based test scoping is enabled by default for Post Upgrade Tests, while still allowing customers to modify the proposed scope in the Manage Upgrade Test app if needed.
  • Simplifying Test Data Management with the Creation of Multiple Data Variants and Assigning them to TDC Variants
    • New Dataset entity for simplified test data management: A new Dataset feature groups multiple Test Data Container (TDC) variants into a reusable entity, making test data easier to organize and manage.
    • Create multiple data variants in one step: User can create and assign multiple data variants simultaneously, significantly reducing repetitive manual effort and speeding up test plan preparation.
    • Automatic assignment of test data: For test plans with multiple TDCs, selecting a dataset automatically maps the appropriate TDC variants, improving consistency and minimizing errors.
    • Greater efficiency and productivity: The enhancements streamline test data management, reduce manual work, improve organization, and allow testing teams to focus more on validating business processes.
  • Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) Comparator enhancements
    • Compare test executions across releases: The enhanced Post Upgrade Test (PUT) Comparator allows users to compare two completed executions of the same execution variant to quickly identify differences, analyze failures, and validate upgrade readiness.
    • Improved visibility and scalability: Enhanced comparison results and pagination make it easier to review and navigate large test execution datasets with hundreds of matching scope items.
    • Advanced filtering for faster analysis: New filtering capabilities enable users to quickly focus on failed, successful, or impacted scope items, reducing manual review effort.
    • Better usability and navigation: User interface improvements streamline comparison workflows, making Post Upgrade Test analysis faster and more intuitive.

Overall, these enhancements significantly improve testing efficiency, increase data accuracy, reduce manual effort, and boost productivity across post-upgrade test automation workflows. For more details, refer to the blog.

 

Please share your feedback and questions in the comments to this blog post or directly via the feedback function in the SAP Activate road map viewer on SAP for Me.

Run simple – SAP Activate – Accelerate and simplify the adoption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

entry.title: Clearing G/L Line Items Using Processing Rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, efficiency and accuracy in financial management are paramount. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers powerful automated processing rules that simplify and expedite the bank statement reconciliation process, ensuring financial data is accurate and up to date. This blog walks you through how to automatically clear G/L line items in payment documents against bank items using Processing Rules - a capability that significantly reduces manual effort and minimizes the risk of human error.

Note: All images and data referenced in this blog are from SAP internal systems, sample data, or demo systems. Any resemblance to real data is purely coincidental.

Finance teams often face the challenge of manually matching and clearing G/L line items in payment documents against corresponding bank items. Without automation, this process is time-consuming and error-prone, especially in high-volume transaction environments. The key question is: How can G/L line items in a payment document be automatically cleared against the bank item using Processing Rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

When bank statements are imported, the system initially attempts to match open items using configured posting rules and interpretation algorithms. However, if the system fails to match an incoming payment with an open item using predefined posting rules, the remaining bank statement information is checked against custom processing rules. 

Solution: Step-by-Step Configuration Guide
Understanding the Two Core Concepts Before You Begin

Before diving into the steps, it is important to understand the distinction between Posting Rules and Processing Rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

Posting Rules are predefined and preconfigured by SAP and cannot be altered by customers. They are standardized to ensure consistency and compliance.
Processing Rules are fully customizable by customers to meet their specific business needs. This distinction allows for a balance between maintaining system integrity and providing customization options.

Step 1: Open the Manage Processing Rules App

  • Log into your SAP Fiori Launchpad and search for the Manage Processing Rules – For Bank Statements app (also referred to as Manage Bank Statement Reprocessing Rules).
  • This app is where you define the conditions and actions that processing rules will perform on bank statement items.

Step 2: Create a New Processing Rule

  • Click the Create button to open the rule editor.
  • On the Processing Rule tab, fill in the following header information:
    • Description: Enter a clear, descriptive name for the rule (e.g., "Auto-Clear G/L Items – Incoming Payments").
    • Journal Entry Type: Select the appropriate document type (e.g., DZ – Customer Payment).
    • Rule for: Select Incoming Payments or Outgoing Payments as applicable.
    • Action Type: Choose the relevant action type. For clearing G/L line items, select Clear in Sequence – Activates interpretation and allocation algorithms to clear open items.

Step 3: Define Conditions

  • Navigate to the Conditions tab. This is where the rule "matches" the correct bank statement items. [4]
  • Add one or more conditions using the following attributes:
    • Attributes: Categories of data to filter on - examples include Amount, Company Code, Currency, External Transaction, House Bank, Memo Line, Partner IBAN, Partner Name, etc.
    • Operators: Define the type of comparison - examples include Equals, Contains, Between, Greater Than, etc.
    • From/To: Define the value to search for. 
  • Ensure conditions are specific enough so the rule only triggers for the right transactions. It is also recommended to include House Bank and Account ID conditions to restrict the rule to a specific bank account.

Step 4: Configure the Action (Based on Action Type)

Depending on the Action Type selected in Step 2, configure the corresponding action details: 

  • For Clear in Sequence: Choose the Processing Instruction MTCH that dictate how the system locates the open items.
     
    • Configure the Processing Instruction to define how the system searches memo line data against open items.
    • Select the Source Field from the dropdown list. The Source Field field refers to a field in the payment advice item or bank statement item (and also in the bank statement item's subitem), for example, the Journal Entry (Advice) or the Assignment Reference (Bank Statement) field. By determining the source field, you tell the system to use the value in that specific field of the payment advice item or bank statement item, and to look for a matching value in the field of an open item.
    • Select the Use Source Field As from the dropdown list. The Use Source Field as field refers to a field of an open item, for example, the Journal Entry (BELNR), the Reference (XBLNR), or Reference Key (XREF) field of open items. By determining this field, you tell the system to look for matching values in this field.



Note: If you select the Match Date checkbox, the system also compares the dates of the payment advice item and of the open items. If you deselect the Match Date checkbox, the system does not compare any dates, and simply uses the values defined in the Source Field and Use Source Field as fields in order to match items.

Restriction
If you select bank-statement-related fields as the source field in the sequence, you cannot select the Match Date checkbox. You can only select the Match Date checkbox with payment-advice-related fields.

If you select the Intercompany Open Items checkbox, the system also includes intercompany open items in the search for matching open items with the defined values for the Source Field and Use Source Field as fields. If you deselect the Intercompany Open Items checkbox, the system does not include any intercompany open items, and only searches for open items belonging to the company code of the bank statement item.

The sequence that you define here is submitted to the allocation algorithm that is determined in the selected processing instruction. The allocation algorithm then uses this sequence to compare the values in specific fields in payment advice items or bank statement items and open items, and thus, to identify matching items which can then be cleared.

Example

In the Clear in Sequence section, you have selected a processing instruction that contains an allocation algorithm with the correct configurations for payment advice processing, such as the MTCH processing instruction. In the Match Items in Sequence section, you define the following values:


Row Number

Source Field

Use Source Field as

1

Memo Line (Bank Statement Item)

Journal Entry

2

Customer Reference Number (Bank Statement Item)

Reference

The system reads this sequence as follows: First, take the value in the Memo Line field of the bank statement item, and compare it with the value in the Journal Entry field of the open items. If the values match, the bank statement item and open item are matched. If not, proceed with the second row: Take the value in the Customer Reference Number (Bank Statement Item) field of the bank statement item, and compare it with the value in the Reference field of the open items. If the values match, the bank statement item and open item are matched. If not, continue with the next processing instruction, or display the item in the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app for manual processing.

  • Under Open Item type section, select G/L Items. The default value of this field is AP/AR Items. If you want to, you can determine that you want the processing instruction to look for G/L items. If you do select G/L Items, you need to specify a range of G/L accounts that the processing instruction should search to find matching open G/L items to be cleared.
  • Define residual item handling, reason codes, or on-account fallback options as needed. 
  • Click on Apply.

Step 5: Save the Rule

  • Click Create to save the rule. 
  • The rule is now saved and available for manual use in the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app.

Step 6: Automate the Rule

To have the rule apply automatically during bank statement import - without manual intervention - you must activate automation: 

  • Select the saved rule in the list view.
  • Click the Automate Rule button (available in the top-right or via the action menu).
  • In the pop-up dialog, select the Procedure Type:
    • 001 – One-Step Posting: Only Posting Area 1 (PA1 – Bank Accounting) will be posted. The Bank Accounting journal entry is posted to the G/L account mentioned in the processing rule and the main bank G/L account. 
    • 002 – Two-Step Posting: Both PA1 (Bank Accounting) and PA2 (Subledger Accounting) will be posted. The Bank Accounting journal entry posts to the main bank G/L account and the bank subaccount; the Subledger Accounting journal entry posts to the bank subaccount and the AP/AR account.
  • Confirm by clicking Automate Rule.

The rule is now active and will be applied automatically for the next incoming bank statement imports.

Step 7: Verify Processing via the Reprocess Bank Statement Items App

After the next bank statement is imported, verify that the rule was applied correctly:

  • Open the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app.
  • Locate the relevant bank statement item and check the processing log.
  • The log will confirm whether the rule was applied and the journal entries were posted successfully.
  • If no open item is found for a bank statement item, the item will need to be handled manually in this app.

Important Note: Handling Multiple Matching Rules

If multiple processing rules match the same bank statement item, the system stops the application of the processing rule and raises the following error:

"Bnk stmnt X itm Y matches conditions of several rules. No rule applied."

In this case, the normal posting rule assigned to the external transaction code in the SSCUI will be applied instead. To avoid this, ensure your rule conditions are specific and non-overlapping. 

Conclusion

Automating the clearing of G/L line items using Processing Rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is a highly effective way to improve efficiency and accuracy in financial close and bank reconciliation processes. By configuring the Manage Processing Rules – For Bank Statements app with the right conditions, action types, and procedure types, finance teams can significantly reduce manual workload and ensure consistent, accurate postings. [1]

Key takeaways:

  • Posting Rules are SAP-delivered and fixed; Processing Rules are customer-configurable.
  • Use Action Type: Clear in Sequence with the processing instruction MTCH to automatically clear G/L open items.
  • Always activate automation on the rule to ensure it runs during bank statement import.
  • Monitor results via the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app and handle exceptions manually where needed. 

For further reading, refer to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud documentation on Automatic Bank Statement Processing and KBA 3780076 on SAP for Me.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Business AI for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release marks another important step in SAP's journey toward the Autonomous Enterprise. As SAP Business AI continues to evolve, it's no longer just about automating individual tasks. Instead, AI is becoming a trusted collaborator, handling repetitive work, surfacing the right insights, and coordinating increasingly complex business processes.

Think of a modern production line. Every station has its own responsibility, but the real efficiency comes from how smoothly everything works together. SAP Business AI plays a similar role across the enterprise, helping coordinate tasks, surface the right information at the right time, and keep business processes moving without interrupting the people who drive them.

The 2608 release brings this vision to life through innovations across multiple dimensions of enterprise AI. In this blog, we'll explore a selection of highlights spanning Agentic AI, the latest Joule innovations, and embedded AI capabilities across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. From intelligent finance agents and AI-powered workspaces to natural language experiences and embedded business process intelligence, these innovations are designed to make everyday work more intuitive, efficient, and context-aware.

Rather than introducing AI as a standalone capability, these innovations embed intelligence directly into the flow of work, helping finance teams accelerate period-end closing, simplifying compliance management, improving project visibility, and making interactions with SAP software feel more natural than ever before.

Take a look at the short video below to see how these AI-driven innovations are helping organizations work smarter today while bringing SAP's vision of the Autonomous Enterprise to life:

 

Agentic AI highlights

Finance

Receivables & Payables Clearing Agent (planned for 2608.2)

Matching open receivables and payables is often a repetitive and time-consuming task. Finance teams need to manually review invoices, identify matching items, and validate clearing proposals, making the process prone to delays and human error.

The new Receivables and Payables Clearing Agent uses SAP Business AI to analyze open items and generate balanced clearing proposals. The agent provides clear explanations for its matching recommendations, allowing accountants to quickly review, validate, and approve proposals before they are automatically posted as clearing documents.

By automating invoice matching and providing transparent AI recommendations, the Clearing Agent helps finance teams accelerate period-end closing, reduce manual effort, and improve operational efficiency—allowing accountants to spend less time on repetitive reconciliation tasks and more time on value-added financial analysis.

 

 

Journal entry agent (planned for 2608.3)

Creating journal entries is an essential part of every finance team's work, but preparing them from spreadsheet data often involves repetitive manual steps. Mapping fields, validating information, and creating journal entries can take valuable time, especially during busy period-end closing activities.

The new Journal Entry Agent makes this process faster and easier. Simply upload your spreadsheet through Joule, and let the agent analyze the data, map it to the required journal entry fields, and generate journal entry proposals automatically. From there, finance teams can review, adjust, and approve the proposals in the Manage Journal Entry Proposals app before posting them.

By reducing manual effort while keeping users in full control, the Journal Entry Agent helps improve accuracy, accelerate period-end closing, and free finance professionals to spend less time on routine data preparation and more time on delivering valuable financial insights.

 

 

Joule innovation highlights

Joule for Business: Moving to SAP-managed

Getting started with AI should be simple. Today, customers need to activate and configure Joule themselves by setting up the required services, identities, roles, and integrations. While this provides flexibility, it also adds complexity before users can start benefiting from SAP Business AI.

Now, Joule is transitioning from a customer-managed to a SAP-managed experience. Instead of handling the technical setup themselves, customers will simply access Joule through SAP for Me, while SAP takes care of the underlying configuration and lifecycle management. This approach also enables SAP to deliver new Joule innovations and enhancements more quickly, ensuring customers can benefit from the latest AI capabilities as they become available.

By removing setup complexity, SAP-managed Joule allows customers to focus on using AI rather than configuring it. For existing customers, a lightweight migration path is planned, with additional details and timelines to be shared in the future. Customers currently implementing Joule can continue with the existing customer-managed setup and seamlessly migrate when SAP-managed Joule becomes available.

 

 

 

Joule for Consultants

Expert Workspace

As organizations increasingly rely on AI for different business scenarios, keeping AI assistants organized and easy to manage becomes just as important as creating them. Without a structured workspace, it can be difficult to find the right AI expert or maintain specialized knowledge across projects and teams.

Expert Workspace in Joule introduces a more streamlined way to organize and manage AI Experts. Each Expert can be tailored with its own instructions and reference files, enabling more focused, context-aware conversations for specific business scenarios. New search, tagging, and filtering capabilities make it easy to quickly find the right Expert, while improved visibility into attachment storage helps users manage their growing AI workspaces more effectively.

By making AI Experts easier to organize, discover, and reuse, Expert Workspace helps users build specialized AI assistants for different workflows, improving productivity while creating a more scalable and efficient AI experience across the enterprise. 

 

 

Joule Work

As AI becomes part of everyday business, users need more than a chatbot, they need an intelligent workspace that brings together insights, automates routine work, and helps them take action. Switching between applications and manually coordinating tasks slows decision-making and reduces productivity.

With the release 2608, Joule Work introduces a dynamic AI workspace that goes beyond chat. Powered by user intent and business context, Joule Work surfaces the right insights, coordinates AI agents and assistants, and helps users complete business tasks from a single place. Whether asking questions in natural language, working in AI-generated Spaces, or accessing AI-powered workflows across desktop and mobile, users can move seamlessly from insight to action.

By bringing conversations, AI-powered workspaces, and intelligent automation together in one experience, Joule Work helps users make faster decisions, reduce context switching, and focus on the work that drives business value: another important step toward SAP's vision of the Autonomous Enterprise.

 

 

Embedded AI highlights

User Experience

AI-assisted easy fill of SAP Fiori elements-based applications

One of the highlights of this release is the general availability of AI-assisted easy fill.

Business users often receive information through emails, meeting notes, chat messages, or phone conversations. Traditionally, they then have to transfer this information manually into ERP transactions—finding the correct fields, switching between screens, and ensuring every value is entered correctly. AI-assisted easy fill removes much of this manual effort.

By simply describing a business update in natural language, SAP Business AI automatically interprets the information and populates the appropriate fields within the business object. Users can then review the suggested values before confirming them, ensuring both speed and accuracy.

This not only reduces repetitive data entry, but also helps improve data quality, lowers the learning curve for new users, and allows experienced users to complete everyday business processes significantly faster.

 

AI-assisted easy filter

We're also expanding natural language capabilities with AI-assisted easy filter. Filtering business data has traditionally required users to know exactly which filter fields exist and how they should be combined. Now, instead of manually building complex filter criteria, users can simply describe what they're looking for in plain language.

SAP Business AI understands the request and automatically translates it into the appropriate filters, including complex combinations such as amounts, currencies, dates, sorting, and grouping. Recent queries and favorite searches are also available, making frequently used searches even faster.

The result is a more intuitive search experience that helps both occasional and experienced users find business information with less effort and greater confidence.

 

Environment, Health, and Safety

AI-assisted compliance management

Managing compliance obligations often means reviewing lengthy regulations, permits, or corporate policies and manually translating them into operational tasks. It's a time-consuming process that can increase the risk of errors or overlooked obligations.

With the 2608 release, AI-assisted compliance management helps automate this work. Users can upload compliance documents as PDFs, and SAP Business AI automatically identifies relevant requirements, proposes structured compliance obligations, and recommends the corresponding operational tasks—such as periodic inspections, maintenance notifications, or reporting activities. A side-by-side view of the original document and the AI-generated suggestions allows users to quickly validate every recommendation while remaining in full control.

By reducing manual data entry and accelerating the setup of compliance obligations, AI-assisted compliance management helps organizations improve accuracy, reduce compliance risks, and stay audit-ready. Instead of spending time extracting information from lengthy documents, compliance professionals can focus on managing risks and ensuring regulatory compliance.

 

Professional Services

AI-assisted aging work in progress analytics and management

For project-based businesses, keeping track of aging work in progress (WIP) is essential for maintaining healthy cash flow. But identifying overdue WIP, understanding its root causes, and deciding what action to take often requires time-consuming analysis across multiple reports.

AI-assisted aging work in progress analytics and management helps project financial controllers gain these insights faster. Using Joule, users can analyze and visualize WIP aging across defined time buckets, while AI automatically generates executive summaries that highlight critical items and recommend the next steps. Intelligent drill-down navigation and automated billing notifications make it easier to investigate issues and accelerate resolution.

By transforming complex project data into actionable insights, this innovation helps finance and project teams reduce manual analysis, speed up billing cycles, lower cash flow risks, and spend more time making informed decisions instead of creating reports.

 

 

Summary 

🎦Register for our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 Early Release Series! 

Between July 13-17, we are hosting a series of 24 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. On our landing page you can register for the entire series or pick the sessions most relevant to you. 

 

🔎Find all our assets  

The release 2608 showcases more than 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:
